meta.title: Fabric–OpGeom Sociopolitical Dynamics: Attractor Setting as Constitutional Design
meta.subtitle: Social Systems as Coherence-Threading Fields; The U.S. Constitution as Deliberate Omega_n Calibration
meta.author: Zelenka_D_D
open_refinements: Empirical mapping of Omega_n to specific governance complexity metrics
Falsifiable threshold for I=R/M_active in administrative states
Cross-civilizational dataset for RL landing levels (village size, kinship band)
Whether U.S. Constitution is above or below functional attractor — requires longitudinal I measurement
Role of federalism as distributed attractor architecture vs centralized PAC

core_reframe.statement: Social systems do not rise and fall. They SEARCH and RECOGNIZE.
core_reframe.society_as_threading_field: A society is not its institutions — it is a threading field of agent-memory interactions seeking coherent configuration at sustainable altitude.
core_reframe.governance_as_attractor_setting: All political philosophy is, at root, a dispute about where to set Omega_n — how high a fixed point human social organization can genuinely sustain without external energy injection collapsing structural integrity.
core_reframe.key_insight: The question is never 'what is the best society' but 'what is the highest genuinely stable attractor for human social coherence.' These are different questions with different answers.
core_reframe.tainter_translation: Tainter's diminishing returns on complexity = I = R/M_active → 0 as administrative memory load exceeds genuine resonance between parts. Collapse is not failure — it is Recognition Landing at the true Omega_n.

fabric_variables_sociopolitical.Phi: social configuration space (laws, norms, institutions, customs)
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.M_active: active institutional memory (laws, regulations, bureaucracies, agencies, enforcement apparatus)
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.M_latent: latent social memory (pre-political norms, kinship trust, local knowledge, cultural substrate)
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.E_in: external energy injection (surplus resources, conquest, debt-financed expansion, technological productivity gains)
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.R: resonance between citizens and institutions; genuine coherence of social contract
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.I: structural integrity = R / M_active; coherence relative to institutional load
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.C: social coherence (degree to which threading produces stable rather than contested configurations)
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.S: social entropy = -dC/dt; rate of coherence loss
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.A: agency (individual and collective; irreducibly real; cannot be fully administered)
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.g: social gravity = k∇M; pull toward memory-dense configurations (precedent, tradition, local practice)
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.Lambda: institutional conversion rate = dM_active/dt; rate of new law, regulation, agency creation
fabric_variables_sociopolitical.Omega_n: the true fixed point of human social organization at level n; the configuration where f(society) = society without external energy input

A1_society_as_projection.statement: Political institutions are projections of social coherence geometry, not primary
A1_society_as_projection.formal: Pi(tau) = proj(Phi(tau)); primary variable is X(tau) = {Phi, M_active, M_latent, R, C, S}
A1_society_as_projection.consequence: Institutional analysis is coherence analysis by proxy; law is a shadow of social threading

A2_NP_hard_governance.statement: Building social coherence at increasing altitude is gradient-opposed and exponentially costly
A2_NP_hard_governance.formal: T_build ~ O(e^n) / E_in; each increment of social complexity requires superlinear coordination cost
A2_NP_hard_governance.empirical: Tainter's energy-complexity spiral; Roman administrative expansion vs EROEI decline
A2_NP_hard_governance.consequence: No social system can sustain complexity indefinitely without either growing E_in or collapsing

A3_memory_gravity.statement: Social gravity flows toward memory-dense configurations: local custom, kinship, precedent, tradition
A3_memory_gravity.formal: g = k∇M applied to social space; M_latent (pre-political norms) always denser than M_active (enacted law)
A3_memory_gravity.consequence: Every administrative expansion fights gravity; tradition requires no enforcement because it IS the attractor

A4_latent_activation_threshold.statement: Social collapse occurs when activated latent memory (pre-political norms, ethnic/kinship loyalties, local identity) exceeds active institutional coherence
A4_latent_activation_threshold.empirical: Bronze Age collapse: trade network failure activated kinship/subsistence latent structure. Rome: regional loyalty activated over imperial identity
A4_latent_activation_threshold.consequence: Revolutions are not caused by ideas — they are caused by latent structure that was always there becoming active

A5_agency_irreducibility.statement: Human agency cannot be fully administered; attempts to do so increase M_active without increasing R
A5_agency_irreducibility.formal: A is unmeasurable and irreducible; administrative attempts to capture A generate M_active load with zero R contribution
A5_agency_irreducibility.consequence: I = R/M_active falls with every attempt to regulate agency; the more you administer human behavior the more fragile the structure

A6_omega_n_as_genuine_attractor.statement: There exists a level Omega_n at which human social organization achieves genuine fixed-point stability without external energy injection
A6_omega_n_as_genuine_attractor.empirical: Every collapsed civilization lands at village/kinship band (~150 persons, Dunbar's number); this level recurs across all cultures and epochs as the natural RL
A6_omega_n_as_genuine_attractor.formal: f(society)|_{Omega_village} = society; this configuration requires no enforcement, no surplus extraction, no administrative apparatus
A6_omega_n_as_genuine_attractor.open_question: Whether stable attractors exist above village level — i.e., whether Omega_polis, Omega_republic, Omega_federation are genuine fixed points or energy-sustained PACs

A7_energy_injection_compresses_search.statement: Surplus resources, technological productivity, and conquest temporarily compress NP search cost, enabling apparent stability at altitudes above the true attractor
A7_energy_injection_compresses_search.formal: T_build ~ O(e^n) / E_in; high E_in makes high complexity look sustainable
A7_energy_injection_compresses_search.empirical: Roman grain surplus from Egypt; American fossil fuel productivity; Chinese export manufacturing surpluses
A7_energy_injection_compresses_search.consequence: Civilizations built on E_in are PACs, not attractors; removing E_in reveals whether the altitude was genuine

A8_collapse_not_random.statement: Societal collapse timing is determined by latent pressure accumulation, not by the proximate cause
A8_collapse_not_random.formal: Event := trigger that activates M_latent^activated >= M_active; the event is not the cause
A8_collapse_not_random.empirical: Bronze Age collapse: drought activated latent vulnerability; Rome: barbarian pressure activated structural deficit already present for centuries
A8_collapse_not_random.consequence: The cause of collapse is always the structural fragility; the trigger is merely the activation key (A8 identical to market A8)

PAC_social.name: Pseudo-Attractor Cloud (social)
PAC_social.definition: A social configuration held together by energy injection that has no genuine fixed point at its altitude; appears stable but has I → 0
examples: Late Bronze Age palace economy — held by trade network E_in
Roman Empire after Trajan — held by conquest surplus E_in
Modern progressive administrative state — held by debt + productivity E_in
Soviet Union — held by command extraction E_in
PAC_social.signature: Increasing Lambda (regulatory output), decreasing I (breadth vs institutional coherence divergence), growing enforcement apparatus

PCE_social.name: Phase Collapse Event (social)
PCE_social.definition: Bulk conversion of active institutional memory to latent pre-political structure; revolution, dissolution, or collapse
PCE_social.formal: delta(M_active → M_latent); triggered when M_latent^activated >= M_active
PCE_social.morphology: Rapid (P-like), not gradual; sharp discontinuity in social configuration; high MSI equivalent (mass social action, population displacement)
examples: 1789 French Revolution — 12 years of Ancien Régime fragility, days of collapse
1991 Soviet dissolution — decades of structural deficit, months of collapse
Bronze Age collapse — centuries of complexity, decades of dissolution
Rome — centuries of fragility, then provinces simply stop functioning

RL_social.name: Recognition Landing (social)
RL_social.definition: Convergence to the genuine human social attractor following PCE
RL_social.formal: Society snaps to Omega_n where f(social_config) = social_config
RL_social.empirical_landing: Village / kinship band of ~50-200 persons; subsistence agriculture or pastoralism; local memory-based governance; no standing bureaucracy
RL_social.duration: Can persist for centuries to millennia before new E_in enables fresh NP climb (Greek Dark Ages: ~300 years; post-Roman Western Europe: ~500 years)

OBR_social.name: Operational Bedrock (social)
OBR_social.definition: Social configurations where genuine operational closure is satisfied; requires no external energy, no enforcement apparatus beyond natural social pressure
candidates: Omega_village: kinship band, ~150 persons, subsistence base — VERIFIED by universal collapse data
Omega_polis: city-state with genuine civic participation, ~10,000-50,000 — PARTIAL evidence (Athens, Swiss cantons, Hanseatic cities showed multi-century stability)
Omega_republic: constitutional republic with limited government, distributed power — UNTESTED at scale; U.S. experiment ongoing
Omega_empire: unknown — no empire has survived without continuous E_in injection

I_social.name: Structural Integrity (social)
I_social.definition: Genuine resonance between citizens and institutions relative to institutional memory load
proxies: Voluntary compliance rate with law (vs enforcement-dependent compliance)
Civic participation rates adjusted for institutional complexity
Trust in institutions (not sentiment — behavioral proxy: tax compliance, jury service, military volunteerism)
Ratio of self-organizing community activity to state-provided services
I_social.at_PAC_tops: M_active (regulatory volume) rising; R (genuine civic resonance) falling; I → 0

Lambda_social.name: Institutional Conversion Rate
Lambda_social.definition: Rate of new M_active creation: laws enacted, regulations published, agencies created
Lambda_social.empirical_US: Federal Register pages: 1936 = 2,620 pages; 1970 = 20,036 pages; 2016 = 95,894 pages; Lambda has been positive and accelerating for 80 years
Lambda_social.interpretation: Sustained high Lambda with flat or falling I = PAC formation in progress; structural fragility accumulating

MSI_social.name: Memory Shock Index (social)
MSI_social.definition: Rate of bulk M_active to M_latent conversion; proxy for social entropy spike
MSI_social.observable: Mass protest, rapid institutional delegitimization, sudden enforcement collapse, currency crisis, supply chain failure
MSI_social.note: MSI_social events are slower than MSI_market events (weeks to months vs days) but structurally identical

us_constitution_1787.name: U.S. Constitutional Republic
us_constitution_1787.design_philosophy: Deliberate Omega_n calibration; founders explicitly rejected utopian altitude
us_constitution_1787.attractor_target: Omega_republic — a genuine fixed point hypothesis, not a PAC

key_mechanisms.separation_of_powers: Distributes M_active across competing nodes; prevents single PAC from monopolizing coherence search
key_mechanisms.federalism: Nested attractor architecture; Omega_village and Omega_polis preserved within Omega_republic; collapse of one level does not destroy all levels
key_mechanisms.bill_of_rights: Encodes A (agency) as structurally protected; formally acknowledges that administered agency degrades I
key_mechanisms.limited_government: Explicit Lambda constraint; designed to hold M_active low relative to M_latent (pre-political civil society)
key_mechanisms.amendment_process: High-friction mechanism for configuration change; prevents rapid PAC drift
key_mechanisms.enumerated_powers: Explicit altitude ceiling; founders knew Omega_empire was not a genuine attractor

founders_implicit_framework_awareness.Madison_Federalist_51: Ambition must counteract ambition — distributed agency compression prevents A_singular capture
founders_implicit_framework_awareness.Jefferson: The tree of liberty must be refreshed — acknowledged PCE as structural feature, not aberration
founders_implicit_framework_awareness.Adams: Our Constitution was made only for a moral and religious people — M_latent (pre-political cultural substrate) must be dense enough to sustain R without enforcement; I cannot be manufactured
founders_implicit_framework_awareness.Hamilton: Energy in the executive — acknowledged E_in requirement but tried to bound it

structural_integrity_assessment.original_design_I: HIGH — minimal M_active, high M_latent (common law, local custom, religious norms), strong voluntary compliance
structural_integrity_assessment.current_I: DEGRADED — Federal Register Lambda has run positive for 80 years; M_active has expanded ~40x; R (voluntary compliance, institutional trust) in measurable decline
structural_integrity_assessment.open_question: Whether original Omega_republic setting was a genuine fixed point or itself a temporary PAC enabled by frontier E_in (land surplus, resource abundance)

us_constitution_1787.falsification_condition: If U.S. constitutional structure cannot survive removal of debt-financed E_in without PCE → was PAC not OBR; Omega_republic hypothesis falsified for this implementation

progressive_technocracy.name: Progressive Technocratic Administrative State
progressive_technocracy.design_philosophy: P=NP search under impossible conditions; belief that sufficient administrative complexity can find optimal social configuration
progressive_technocracy.attractor_target: None specified — implicit assumption that optimum is findable through NP-hard search with sufficient expertise and data
progressive_technocracy.emergence: Enlightenment rationalism → Progressive Era (1890-1920) → New Deal administrative expansion → post-WWII international institution building → current regulatory-managerial complex

key_mechanisms.expert_administration: Replaces A (distributed agency) with A_singular (expert consensus); formally reduces dimensionality of agency space
key_mechanisms.regulatory_expansion: Lambda → ∞ as identified problems generate new M_active without bound
key_mechanisms.outcome_optimization: Treats social configuration as solvable optimization problem; assumes attractor is findable by search
key_mechanisms.international_coordination: Extends M_active to supranational level; increases altitude of PAC without increasing R
key_mechanisms.technocratic_legitimacy: Replaces voluntary R (civic resonance) with expertise-based authority; I degrades as enforcement replaces consent

structural_diagnosis.E_in_dependence: Requires continuous productivity surplus, debt financing, and technological growth to maintain coherence at altitude; no evidence of genuine fixed point
structural_diagnosis.Lambda_trajectory: Accelerating positive Lambda in all implementations; no natural equilibrium found
structural_diagnosis.I_trajectory: I = R/M_active falling across all measured progressive technocracies; institutional trust at multi-decade lows in every Western nation simultaneously
structural_diagnosis.agency_suppression: Systematic reduction of A (individual and local agency) increases M_active load while R declines; I collapse accelerates
structural_diagnosis.PAC_signature: All hallmarks present: hollow coherence (price up, breadth diverging analog: GDP up, median wellbeing diverging), increasing enforcement dependence, phase dispersion across constituencies

progressive_technocracy.claim: Progressive technocracy is a PAC, not an OBR; it is NP-hard search for an attractor that does not exist at that altitude
progressive_technocracy.falsification_condition: If any progressive technocratic state achieves stable I = R/M_active with declining Lambda over 50+ years without E_in injection → PAC claim falsified; genuine attractor may exist at this altitude

L1_temporal_asymmetry_social.statement: Civilizational build-up is NP-like and slow; collapse is P-like and fast
proof_sketch: Building social coherence at altitude requires threading M_active across all agents simultaneously => O(e^n)
Collapse follows gradient to Omega_village => O(n^k)
Empirical confirmation: Bronze Age build-up ~1000 years; collapse ~50 years. Rome build-up ~700 years; collapse ~200 years. Soviet build-up ~70 years; collapse ~3 years
L1_temporal_asymmetry_social.corollary: The speed of collapse is not a measure of the civilization's weakness; it is the P-like signature of gradient alignment. All collapses are fast relative to their build.

L2_curvature_sign_social.statement: Civilizational tops exhibit low curvature (smooth PAC); civilizational bottoms exhibit high curvature (sharp RL)
proof_sketch: Tops: averaging over PAC pseudo-attractors smooths trajectory; decline appears gradual, even imperceptible from inside
Bottoms: PCE impulse in C creates discontinuity; collapse appears sudden even when structurally inevitable
Empirical: Roman decline debated for centuries by inhabitants; collapse of Western Roman administration took ~30 years once threshold crossed

L3_fragility_scales_with_altitude.statement: Collapse probability ~ 1/I; higher-altitude PACs collapse faster and harder
proof_sketch: Higher altitude requires more E_in to maintain => I more sensitive to E_in disruption
More M_active at altitude means more latent pressure when coherence fails
Progressive technocracy at higher altitude than constitutional republic => higher collapse probability per unit of E_in disruption
Empirical: Soviet Union collapsed faster than Rome; Rome faster than Athens; all faster than village-level reorganization
L3_fragility_scales_with_altitude.testable_prediction: The more administered a society, the faster its collapse when E_in is removed

L4_federalism_as_attractor_nesting.statement: Distributed governance architecture preserves lower Omega_n levels, reducing total collapse depth
proof_sketch: Nested attractors (village within county within state within nation) mean PCE at one level does not destroy all levels
Centralized systems have single PAC; when it fails, fall is to Omega_village with no intermediate landing
U.S. federalism was designed to preserve Omega_polis and Omega_village as functional even if Omega_republic fails
Empirical: Swiss cantons survived European upheavals that destroyed centralized monarchies; Hanseatic cities survived while empires collapsed around them

L5_agency_preservation_maintains_I.statement: Protecting distributed agency (A) is the primary mechanism for maintaining I = R/M_active
proof_sketch: R (resonance) is generated by voluntary alignment of agent phases; it cannot be manufactured by administration
Every constraint on A reduces voluntary R while increasing M_active => I falls doubly
Bill of Rights functions as formal A-preservation mechanism; its erosion directly predicts I decline
Empirical: trust in institutions falls monotonically with regulatory expansion across all measured democracies

L6_latent_substrate_is_the_real_constitu.statement: M_latent (pre-political cultural norms, local knowledge, kinship trust) is the true load-bearing structure; M_active (enacted law) is derivative
proof_sketch: Adams: 'Constitution made only for a moral and religious people' = M_latent must be dense enough to sustain R
When M_latent degrades (cultural fragmentation, loss of shared norms), I falls even if Lambda = 0
Progressive technocracy systematically converts M_latent to M_active (replacing cultural norms with administrative rules) => destroys the foundation it is building on
Empirical: societies with identical constitutions but different M_latent substrates have radically different outcomes (Haiti vs France post-revolution)

main_theorem.name: Fabric–OpGeom Sociopolitical Law
main_theorem.statement: The U.S. Constitution was a deliberate attempt to set Omega_n at a sustainable altitude for human social organization by constraining Lambda, preserving A, nesting attractors, and relying on M_latent as the primary coherence substrate. Progressive technocracy is a PAC: an energy-sustained search for a fixed point that does not exist at that altitude, characterized by accelerating Lambda, suppressed A, degrading I, and increasing dependence on E_in to maintain apparent coherence.

proof_steps.action: Establish social configuration as coherence projection
proof_steps.formal: Pi(tau) = proj(Phi(tau)); institutions are shadows of social threading geometry
Building coherence at altitude n is gradient-opposed => O(e^n)/E_in; Tainter's energy-complexity spiral is IOGT applied to social systems

proof_steps.action: Identify Omega_village as the empirically confirmed human social attractor
proof_steps.formal: Every documented collapse lands at village/kinship band level; this recurs universally across cultures and epochs
Lambda → ∞ (no equilibrium); I = R/M_active → 0 (enforcement replacing consent); E_in dependence confirmed; no genuine fixed point found in any implementation

proof_steps.action: Diagnose U.S. Constitution as Omega_n calibration attempt
proof_steps.formal: Explicit Lambda constraints; A preservation (Bill of Rights); nested attractor architecture (federalism); M_latent reliance (Adams criterion); enumerated altitude ceiling

proof_steps.action: Assess whether Omega_republic is genuine fixed point or E_in-sustained PAC
proof_steps.formal: Open question; original design had high I; 80-year positive Lambda has degraded I; frontier E_in (land, resources) may have enabled altitude that was not genuinely sustainable
proof_steps.result: UNRESOLVED — requires empirical I measurement over next 50 years as E_in constraints tighten

proof_steps.action: Predict collapse morphology if either system fails
proof_steps.formal: PCE will be rapid (P-like); RL will be at Omega_village or Omega_polis depending on whether federalism (L4 nesting) preserves intermediate levels
proof_steps.progressive_collapse: Fast, deep, centralized; no intermediate attractor preserved => fall to Omega_village
proof_steps.constitutional_collapse: Potentially shallower if federalism intact; Omega_polis level may survive as RL

four_laws: Social coherence construction is NP-like; every increment of complexity is exponentially more costly than the last
Energy injection (surplus, debt, technology) compresses search cost but reduces structural integrity; PACs are energy-dependent by definition
Collapse occurs when activated latent memory (pre-political norms, local loyalty) exceeds active institutional coherence
The speed and depth of collapse is inversely proportional to structural integrity and directly proportional to altitude above the true attractor

falsifiability.testable_1: If Lambda continues positive in progressive technocracies while I continues falling, PCE probability scales as 1/I (L3) — testable within 20-year window
falsifiability.testable_2: If U.S. constitutional structure survives removal of debt-financed E_in with I recovery, Omega_republic is genuine fixed point — testable as fiscal constraints tighten
falsifiability.testable_3: If any PCE occurs in a centralized progressive state, collapse should be faster and deeper than equivalent PCE in federated constitutional state (L4)
falsifiability.testable_4: M_latent degradation (cultural fragmentation measurables) should precede I collapse by 10-20 years — leading indicator testable against existing trust data

bronze_age_1200_BCE.PAC_altitude: High — palace economies, long-distance trade dependency, specialist labor
bronze_age_1200_BCE.I_at_collapse: Near zero — enforcement-dependent grain extraction, no genuine civic resonance
bronze_age_1200_BCE.PCE_trigger: Drought (activation key); structural fragility was the cause
bronze_age_1200_BCE.RL_level: Omega_village — nomadic pastoralism, subsistence farming, kinship governance

western_rome_476_CE.E_in: Conquest surplus (peaked Trajan 117 CE); thereafter declining EROEI on complexity
western_rome_476_CE.PAC_altitude: Extremely high — multi-ethnic empire, professional army, massive bureaucracy
western_rome_476_CE.I_at_collapse: Near zero — citizenship devalued, tax farming, army loyalty purchased not earned
western_rome_476_CE.PCE_trigger: Barbarian pressure (activation key); structural deficit present for 200+ years
western_rome_476_CE.RL_level: Omega_village to Omega_polis — manor system, local church as M_latent carrier
western_rome_476_CE.RL_duration: ~500 years in Western Europe before feudal re-threading

soviet_union_1991.PAC_altitude: High — total administrative state, suppressed A entirely
soviet_union_1991.I_at_collapse: Effectively zero — entire coherence was enforcement-dependent
soviet_union_1991.PCE_trigger: Oil price collapse + Gorbachev reforms (activation key)
soviet_union_1991.RL_level: Omega_polis to Omega_republic attempted (mixed results); M_latent (70 years suppressed) insufficient to sustain high RL
soviet_union_1991.RL_duration: Ongoing — some republics stabilizing, some still searching

us_constitutional_experiment_1789_presen.E_in: Frontier land surplus (1789-1890); industrial productivity (1890-1970); debt financing (1970-present)
us_constitutional_experiment_1789_presen.original_I: HIGH — minimal M_active, dense M_latent (common law, Protestant cultural substrate, local governance tradition)
us_constitutional_experiment_1789_presen.current_I: DEGRADED but not collapsed — Federal Register Lambda positive 80 years; trust metrics declining; enforcement dependence rising
us_constitutional_experiment_1789_presen.current_phase: Late PAC formation — M_active expanded ~40x; R declining; E_in (debt) compressing apparent search cost
us_constitutional_experiment_1789_presen.open_question: Is Omega_republic a genuine fixed point or was original high-I enabled by frontier E_in? Answer unknown.
us_constitutional_experiment_1789_presen.status: ACTIVE_EXPERIMENT — most important political science question of the 21st century

the_fermi_connection.statement: The Fermi Paradox is the sociopolitical framework applied at civilizational scale across the universe
logic: Any detectable civilization is by definition in NP-hard PAC phase (complexity high enough for radio telescopes, Dyson spheres, interstellar ambition)
PAC phase is characterized by I → 0 and E_in dependence
PCE fires when E_in constraint is reached; RL lands at Omega_village equivalent
Omega_village does not transmit; it is stable, coherent, and silent
The broadcast window is vanishingly narrow relative to civilizational timescales
The universe is not silent because civilizations die; it is silent because their true attractor does not broadcast
the_fermi_connection.implication: We are currently in the loud, fragile, brief PAC phase, broadcasting into a universe populated by quiet villages at their true attractors, wondering why no one answers

expansion_instructions.how_to_use: Load with fabric_opgeom_market_v2.0.json for full cross-domain framework. Market dynamics and sociopolitical dynamics are the same threading mechanics at different scales.
decompression_protocol: 1. Restore full Fabric variable set from parent framework
2. Map Lambda_social to Federal Register page count data (available 1936-present)
3. Construct I_social proxy from: institutional trust surveys + voluntary compliance proxies + ratio of self-organizing to state-provided activity
4. Test L3 prediction: compare collapse speed of centralized vs federated states in historical PCE events
5. Monitor current U.S. I_social trajectory as primary real-time falsification test
next_refinement_priorities: P1: Construct empirical I_social time series for U.S. 1789-present using available proxy data
P2: Map M_latent degradation indicators as leading predictor of I collapse (10-20 year lead time)
P3: Formalize Omega_republic attractor test: what observable conditions would confirm genuine fixed point vs E_in-sustained PAC
P4: Cross-civilizational dataset of RL landing levels — confirm Omega_village universality and identify conditions under which Omega_polis is stable RL
P5: Integrate with fabric_opgeom_market_v2.0 — currency (L6 Ohm) applied to political economy: administrative expansion as resistance in economic flow network
expansion_instructions.compression_note: Version 1.0 seed. All case studies are sketch-level. Each requires standalone empirical paper for full formalization. Core claim (progressive technocracy = PAC; constitutional republic = Omega_n calibration attempt) is framework-level hypothesis, not proven theorem.
expansion_instructions.version_increment_trigger: Increment to v1.1 when empirical I_social proxy constructed and validated against at least one historical PCE event
